chapter_title.ar: بَابُ فَصْلِ مَا بَيْنَ صَيْدِ الْبَرِّ وَ الْبَحْرِ وَ مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمُحْرِمِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 112 – Difference between the prey of the land and the sea, and what is Permissible for the one in Ihraam from that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/387)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/387)
text: الْمُحْرِمُ يَتَنَكَّبُ الْجَرَادَ إِذَا كَانَ عَلَى الطَّرِيقِ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ بُدّاً فَقَتَلَ فَلَا شَيْ‏ءَ عَلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad, from Hareyz, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5th or 6th Imamasws having said: ‘The one in Ihraam steps upon the locusts when they are upon the road, so if he cannot find it apparent, so he kills it, then there is nothing upon him’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad from Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “One of the two Imam, (abu Ja’far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘A person in the state of Ihram should avoid locusts, if they are on the road but if it is not avoidable and they get killed, then there is nothing on him.’”
